multiplechoiceresponse.label: A lending dataset is defined to be α-biased if the difference in loan-approval rates between Group A and Group B is at least α = 0.20. The dataset shows a loan approval rate of 65% for Group A and 50% for Group B. Which statement is correct?

choicegroup.choice: The dataset is not α-biased because the difference (0.15) is smaller than α = 0.20
The dataset is α-biased because any difference in rates indicates bias.
The dataset is α-biased because lending is a high-stakes domain.
The dataset is α-biased because removing protected attributes cannot fix correlation.

div.p: A dataset is defined to be α‑biased when the difference in positive outcome rates between two groups is at least α. Using this definition and the examples from Lecture 2, α-bias is threshold-based: difference ≥ α. Here the difference in positive outcome rates is 0.65 − 0.50 = 0.15. Because 0.15 < 0.20, the dataset does not meet the criterion for α-bias at α = 0.20. High-stakes context and correlation issues may matter for broader fairness concerns, but α-bias specifically is determined by whether the outcome-rate gap crosses the chosen α threshold.
